reasoning: Kenvue (KVUE) is being acquired by Kimberly-Clark (KMB) in a cash-and-stock deal ($3.50 + 0.14625 KMB shares). Recently, KMB experienced an 11% rally in two weeks, dragging KVUE from $17.00 on Jan 29 up to $18.70 on Feb 13. According to technical indicators and the time-series forecasting tool, this steep run puts the stock into overbought territory with a high likelihood of a short-term pullback (linear model predicts P(>18.7) = 0.45). Therefore, the near-term probability of closing higher than the recent peak of 18.70 is below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends to March, the built-in positive drift from the narrowing merger arbitrage spread and standard equity risk premium offset some of the mean-reversion risk. In alignment with increasing uncertainty over a longer time horizon, the probability converges closer to the 0.5 baseline for the later resolution date.
